questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Yield to traffic on your left, then go
questions.explanation: A steady red light always means come to a full stop and stay stopped until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means you should:
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe, as you would at a stop sign
Speed up to clear the intersection
Continue without stopping
Stop and wait for the light to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
The light is about to turn green
Pedestrians have the right-of-way only
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop when you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What does an eight-sided (octagonal) red sign always mean?
choices: Yield
Stop
Do not enter
Speed limit ahead
questions.explanation: The octagonal red sign is reserved exclusively for stop signs.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign is used to:
choices: Give directions to a city
Mark a construction zone
Warn of hazards or changing road conditions ahead
Show a regulatory speed limit
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert drivers to hazards ahead.

questions.prompt: What color are signs that mark construction and work zones?
choices: Orange
Green
Blue
Brown
questions.explanation: Orange signs identify construction and work zones where extra caution is required.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: You may pass when traffic is clear
You may not pass
The lane is ending
Two-way left-turn lane
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited.

questions.prompt: What do white lane lines indicate?
choices: They separate traffic moving in opposite directions
They separate lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
They mark the edge of the road only
They always prohibit lane changes
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
choices: Stop completely before proceeding
Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is broken; treat it as a stop sign
Pedestrians may not cross
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells drivers to slow down and proceed carefully.

questions.prompt: What does a green guide sign typically display?
choices: Warnings about hazards
Directions, distances, and destinations
Construction information
Stop and yield instructions
questions.explanation: Green signs provide guidance such as directions, distances, and destinations.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two vehicles arrive at exactly the same time. Who has the right-of-way?
choices: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Only vehicles behind you
Oncoming traffic and pedestrians crossing the road you are entering
No one if you have a green light
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians in the path of the turn.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, a right turn on a red light is:
choices: Never allowed
Allowed after a complete stop, unless a sign prohibits it, once traffic and pedestrians are clear
Allowed without stopping
Allowed only between 6 a.m. and midnight
questions.explanation: Nebraska permits right turns on red after a full stop, as long as no sign forbids it and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a roadway from a private driveway should:
choices: Proceed first because they are moving slower
Yield to all traffic already on the roadway
Honk and merge immediately
Expect oncoming traffic to stop
questions.explanation: Drivers entering from a driveway or alley must yield to vehicles already on the road.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and sirens on, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is waiting to cross at a marked crosswalk. You should:
choices: Continue if you have the green light
Yield and let the pedestrian cross
Honk so they wait
Speed up to pass before they step out
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield the right-of-way to pedestrians in or entering a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When two vehicles approach an uncontrolled intersection at roughly the same time, who yields?
choices: The vehicle on the right yields to the vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the left yields to the vehicle on the right
Whichever vehicle is going straight
Neither, both may proceed
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: Before turning, when should you signal?
choices: Only if other cars are nearby
At least 100 feet before the turn
After you begin turning
Signaling is optional in town
questions.explanation: You should signal continuously for at least 100 feet before turning to warn others of your intentions.

questions.prompt: At a roundabout, you should:
choices: Enter quickly to keep traffic moving
Yield to traffic already circulating in the roundabout
Stop in the middle to let others in
Travel in the opposite direction of the arrows
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roundabout must yield to traffic already moving within it.

questions.prompt: When you want to merge onto a highway from an on-ramp, you should:
choices: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Adjust your speed to match traffic and yield to vehicles already on the highway
Force your way into the nearest lane
Drive slowly in the merge lane
questions.explanation: Match the speed of highway traffic and yield to vehicles already in the lane before merging.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the speed limit in a Nebraska residential district?
choices: 15 mph
25 mph
35 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Nebraska's default residential district speed limit is 25 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum speed limit on a Nebraska rural interstate highway, unless otherwise posted?
choices: 55 mph
65 mph
75 mph
85 mph
questions.explanation: Nebraska allows speeds up to 75 mph on rural interstate highways where posted.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a Nebraska business district is:
choices: 20 mph
30 mph
40 mph
50 mph
questions.explanation: Nebraska's default business district speed limit is 20 mph unless otherwise posted.

questions.prompt: The three-second rule is used to:
choices: Time how long a yellow light lasts
Determine a safe following distance behind the vehicle ahead
Measure how fast you can react to a sign
Decide when to change a tire
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you keep a safe following distance behind the car in front of you.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or heavy traffic, you should:
choices: Decrease your following distance
Increase your following distance
Keep the same following distance
Follow as closely as possible to see better
questions.explanation: Poor conditions require more space, so increase your following distance well beyond three seconds.

questions.prompt: Even when the posted limit is higher, Nebraska law requires you to drive:
choices: At exactly the speed limit at all times
At a speed that is reasonable and prudent for current conditions
As fast as traffic around you
At least 10 mph below the limit
questions.explanation: You must always drive at a reasonable and prudent speed for conditions, even below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect your total stopping distance?
choices: It has no effect
It increases the distance needed to stop
It decreases the distance needed to stop
It only affects braking distance, not reaction distance
questions.explanation: Higher speeds increase both reaction and braking distance, lengthening the total distance to stop.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is dangerous because it:
choices: Saves fuel
Can block traffic flow and cause crashes
Is always legal and safe
Improves your reaction time
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly can impede traffic and lead to collisions, so keep pace with safe traffic flow.

questions.prompt: Before entering a curve, you should:
choices: Brake hard in the middle of the curve
Slow down before entering the curve
Speed up to maintain control
Coast in neutral
questions.explanation: Reduce speed before a curve so you can maintain control without braking hard within it.

questions.prompt: A safe following distance gives you:
choices: More time to react and stop if traffic ahead slows
Permission to text
A reason to speed up
Less visibility of the road
questions.explanation: Adequate following distance gives you time to perceive and respond to changes in traffic ahead.

questions.prompt: You may NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: A broken yellow line is on your side
A solid yellow line is on your side
You are on a clear, straight road
The road has two lanes in your direction
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the road means passing is not permitted.

questions.prompt: When passing another vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: Immediately after pulling alongside
You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
The other driver waves you over
You reach the next intersection
questions.explanation: Return to your lane only after you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror to leave it safe room.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, slower traffic should:
choices: Use the left lane
Stay in the right lanes
Drive in the center turn lane
Straddle two lanes
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles should keep to the right, leaving the left lane open for passing.

questions.prompt: A center two-way left-turn lane may be used:
choices: For passing slower cars
Only to make or prepare for a left turn
As an extra travel lane
For parking
questions.explanation: The center two-way left-turn lane is for turning left, not for through travel or passing.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Only check your rearview mirror
Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder at your blind spot
Change lanes quickly without signaling
Honk your horn
questions.explanation: A safe lane change requires signaling and checking both mirrors and your blind spot.

questions.prompt: Passing is generally unsafe and prohibited:
choices: On a long, straight, open highway
On hills and curves where you cannot see far enough ahead
In a passing zone with a broken yellow line
On a multilane one-way street
questions.explanation: You should never pass on hills or curves because oncoming traffic may be hidden from view.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Passing is never allowed
Passing is allowed when it is safe
The road is one-way
You must stop
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates that passing is permitted when the road ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: When a lane you are in is ending, you should:
choices: Wait until the very end, then force in
Merge early and smoothly into the open lane
Stop and wait for a gap
Drive on the shoulder
questions.explanation: Merge early and smoothly when a lane ends rather than waiting until the last moment.

questions.prompt: On most roads, you should drive in which lane except when passing?
choices: The left lane
The right lane
The center turn lane
Any lane you prefer
questions.explanation: Keep right except when passing, leaving the left lane available for overtaking.

questions.prompt: Under Nebraska's Move Over Law, when approaching a stopped vehicle with flashing lights you should:
choices: Maintain speed and hold your lane
Slow down and, when possible, move over to a lane farther away
Speed up to pass quickly
Stop in your lane
questions.explanation: Nebraska's Move Over Law requires slowing down and changing lanes away from stopped vehicles showing warning lights.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
choices: They are louder
They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
They always travel faster
They have brighter lights
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small size makes it easy to overlook, so check carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When driving near a large truck, remember that trucks:
choices: Can stop faster than cars
Have large blind spots and need more room to stop and turn
Never need extra space
Can see directly behind them
questions.explanation: Large trucks have big blind spots and require extra distance to stop and turn safely.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Pass as closely as possible
Give them a safe passing distance and pass when clear
Honk to move them off the road
Speed up and cut back in front quickly
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the same road rights as drivers and need a safe passing distance.

questions.prompt: When you see slow-moving farm equipment displaying a reflective orange triangle, you should:
choices: Pass immediately, regardless of conditions
Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
Tailgate to encourage them to speed up
Drive on the shoulder around it
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so slow down and pass only when you have a safe, legal opportunity.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on Nebraska roads:
choices: Must always ride on the sidewalk
Have the same rights and responsibilities as drivers
Have no legal right to use the road
May ignore traffic signals
questions.explanation: Bicyclists generally have the same rights and duties as motor vehicle drivers.

questions.prompt: When approaching a stopped school bus with flashing red lights on a two-lane road, you must:
choices: Slow down and pass carefully
Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Honk and continue
Pass on the left only
questions.explanation: Flashing red lights on a school bus mean all traffic must stop for children loading or unloading.

questions.prompt: A motorcycle is entitled to:
choices: Only half a lane
Use a full lane of traffic
Ride on the shoulder only
Be passed within the same lane
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, so do not crowd or share their lane.

questions.prompt: Nebraska's expanded Move Over Law now requires you to slow down or move over for:
choices: Only police cars
Any stopped vehicle, including disabled cars, cyclists, and farm equipment
Only ambulances
Vehicles moving in the opposite direction
questions.explanation: Nebraska expanded the Move Over Law to cover any stopped vehicle, including disabled cars, pedestrians, cyclists, and farm equipment.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, a driver age 21 or older is legally intoxicated at a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of:
choices: 0.05 percent
0.08 percent
0.10 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: For drivers 21 and older, the legal BAC limit in Nebraska is 0.08 percent.

questions.prompt: Under Nebraska's zero-tolerance law, a driver under age 21 may not drive with a BAC of:
choices: 0.08 percent or more
0.05 percent or more
0.02 percent or more
0.10 percent or more
questions.explanation: Nebraska's zero-tolerance law prohibits drivers under 21 from driving with a BAC of 0.02 percent or more.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that will sober up a person who has been drinking?
choices: Coffee
A cold shower
Time
Fresh air
questions.explanation: Only time allows the body to remove alcohol; coffee, showers, and air do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: Nebraska law prohibits drivers from using a handheld device to:
choices: Listen to music
Read, type, or send a text or written message
Use hands-free navigation
Charge the phone
questions.explanation: It is illegal to use a handheld device to read, type, or send a written message while driving.

questions.prompt: How can prescription or over-the-counter medications affect driving?
choices: They never affect driving
They can cause drowsiness and slow reaction time
They always improve alertness
They only matter for drivers over 65
questions.explanation: Many medications can impair driving by causing drowsiness or slowing reaction time.

questions.prompt: The safest way to avoid impaired driving is to:
choices: Drive slowly after drinking
Never drive after consuming alcohol or drugs
Drink only beer before driving
Open the windows while driving
questions.explanation: The only sure way to avoid impaired driving is to not drive after using alcohol or drugs.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes:
choices: Keeping both hands on the wheel
Texting, eating, or other activities that take attention from driving
Scanning the road ahead
Checking mirrors
questions.explanation: Anything that takes your attention from driving, such as texting or eating, is a distraction.

questions.prompt: If you must make a phone call while driving, the safest action is to:
choices: Hold the phone while steering
Pull over safely and stop before using the device
Dial quickly at a red light
Use your knees to steer
questions.explanation: The safest choice is to pull over and stop before using a phone.

questions.prompt: Alcohol primarily affects a driver's:
choices: Tire pressure
Judgment, coordination, and reaction time
Fuel economy
Headlight brightness
questions.explanation: Alcohol impairs judgment, coordination, and reaction time, all critical to safe driving.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, when must you turn on your headlights?
choices: Only in heavy traffic
From sunset to sunrise and when you cannot see persons or vehicles 500 feet ahead
Only on interstates
Only when other cars have their lights on
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires headlights from sunset to sunrise and whenever visibility drops below 500 feet.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, you should turn on your headlights whenever you use your:
choices: Radio
Windshield wipers in poor weather
Air conditioning
Cruise control
questions.explanation: When weather forces you to use your wipers, you should also turn on your headlights.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
choices: High-beam headlights
Low-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights only while moving
questions.explanation: Low beams work best in fog because high beams reflect off the fog and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Slam on the brakes
Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: To recover from a skid, ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go.

questions.prompt: Which road surfaces freeze first in cold weather?
choices: Gravel roads
Bridges and overpasses
Parking lots
Tunnels
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other roadways because cold air surrounds them on all sides.

questions.prompt: When driving on wet or icy roads, you should:
choices: Brake hard and steer quickly
Slow down and avoid sudden braking or steering
Increase your speed
Follow closely to see better
questions.explanation: On slippery roads, reduce speed and make gentle, gradual movements to keep traction.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning occurs when:
choices: Tires lose contact with the road and ride on a film of water
The engine overheats
Brakes get too cold
Headlights are too bright
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when tires ride up on water and lose contact with the road surface.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in heavy rain and visibility is very poor, you should:
choices: Turn off your headlights
Slow down, turn on your low beams, and increase following distance
Speed up to get through it
Use your high beams
questions.explanation: In heavy rain, slow down, use low beams, and leave extra space to stop safely.

questions.prompt: When the sun's glare makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Speed up to get past it
Use your sun visor, slow down, and increase following distance
Close your eyes briefly
Turn off your headlights
questions.explanation: Glare reduces visibility, so use your visor, slow down, and keep extra space ahead.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Facing downhill, turn your wheels toward the curb so the car rolls into it if it moves.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb so the tires rest against it
Straight ahead
Toward the center of the road
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the tires catch the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: In a marked parking space
In front of a fire hydrant or blocking a crosswalk
On a residential street
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a hydrant or blocking a crosswalk is prohibited because it blocks access and safety.

questions.prompt: After parking on any hill, you should always:
choices: Leave the car in neutral
Set the parking brake and shift into park
Leave the engine running
Turn the wheels straight
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake and shift into park to keep a parked vehicle from rolling.

questions.prompt: Before pulling out of a parallel parking space, you should:
choices: Pull out quickly without looking
Signal, check mirrors and blind spots, and yield to traffic
Back up onto the sidewalk
Honk and go
questions.explanation: Leaving a parking space safely requires signaling, checking for traffic, and yielding before pulling out.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for persons with disabilities:
choices: Anytime for a few minutes
Only if you have a valid disability permit or plate
If no other spaces are open
While you wait for a passenger
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces may be used only by vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: You should never stop or park:
choices: In a designated parking lot
Within an intersection or on a crosswalk
On a wide shoulder during an emergency
In your own driveway
questions.explanation: Stopping or parking in an intersection or on a crosswalk blocks traffic and pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Curb colors and posted signs at a parking spot:
choices: Can be ignored if you park briefly
Must be obeyed because they indicate parking rules
Apply only to commercial vehicles
Are only suggestions
questions.explanation: Posted signs and curb markings define where and when parking is allowed and must be obeyed.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a Nebraska Learner's Permit (LPD)?
choices: 14 years
15 years
16 years
18 years
questions.explanation: In Nebraska, you may apply for a Learner's Permit (LPD) at age 15.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must a teen complete before getting a Provisional Operator's Permit?
choices: 20 hours
30 hours
50 hours
60 hours
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires 50 hours of certified supervised driving, including at least 10 at night, before a POP.

questions.prompt: Of the required supervised driving hours, how many must be at night?
choices: At least 5 hours
At least 10 hours
At least 20 hours
None are required at night
questions.explanation: At least 10 of the 50 supervised hours must be completed at night (between sunset and sunrise).

questions.prompt: A Learner's Permit holder must drive while accompanied by:
choices: Any passenger with a phone
A licensed driver at least 21 years old
Another teen with a permit
No one is required
questions.explanation: Learner's Permit holders must always be supervised by a licensed driver who is at least 21.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with a Provisional Operator's Permit, the holder may carry:
choices: No restrictions on passengers
Only one passenger under age 19 who is not an immediate family member
Up to four teen passengers
Only adult passengers
questions.explanation: For the first six months, a POP holder may carry only one non-family passenger under age 19.

questions.prompt: Unless an exception applies, a Provisional Operator's Permit holder may not drive between:
choices: 10 p.m. and 5 a.m.
Midnight and 6 a.m.
9 p.m. and 7 a.m.
11 p.m. and 4 a.m.
questions.explanation: A POP holder generally may not drive from midnight to 6 a.m. except for work, school activities, or with a licensed adult.

questions.prompt: To obtain a Learner's Permit in Nebraska, you must pass:
choices: Only a driving test
A written knowledge test and a vision screening
Only a vision screening
A physical fitness test
questions.explanation: A Learner's Permit requires passing the written knowledge test and a vision screening.

questions.prompt: The Nebraska written knowledge test for a Class O permit consists of how many questions?
choices: 20 questions
25 questions
40 questions
50 questions
questions.explanation: Nebraska's Class O knowledge test has 25 questions, and you must answer at least 20 correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, accumulating how many points within a two-year period results in license revocation?
choices: 6 points
9 points
12 points
15 points
questions.explanation: Collecting 12 or more points within two years leads to revocation of your Nebraska driver's license.

questions.prompt: Under Nebraska's implied consent law, refusing a lawful chemical test results in:
choices: A warning only
Automatic license revocation
A small parking fine
No consequences
questions.explanation: By driving in Nebraska you consent to chemical testing, and refusal brings automatic license revocation.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, all children age 6 through 17 must:
choices: Ride only in the back seat
Wear a seat belt in any seat of the vehicle
Sit in a booster seat
Be restrained only on highways
questions.explanation: Nebraska law requires children age 6 through 17 to be belted in any seating position.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, children up to what age must ride in a rear-facing child restraint until they outgrow it?
choices: Up to age 1
Up to age 2
Up to age 4
Up to age 6
questions.explanation: Children up to age 2 must use a rear-facing child restraint until they exceed its height or weight limits.

questions.prompt: In Nebraska, a child passenger restraint system is required for children:
choices: Up to age 4
Up to age 6
Up to age 8
Up to age 12
questions.explanation: Nebraska requires a proper child passenger restraint system for children up to age 8.

questions.prompt: Points are added to a Nebraska driving record for offenses such as:
choices: Parking in a paid lot
Speeding and reckless driving
Buying fuel
Washing your car
questions.explanation: Moving violations like speeding and reckless driving add points to your driving record.

questions.prompt: The purpose of Nebraska's point system is to:
choices: Reward fast drivers
Track traffic violations and identify high-risk drivers
Lower insurance for everyone
Replace driving tests
questions.explanation: The point system tracks violations so that repeat or high-risk drivers can lose driving privileges.

questions.prompt: Driving without a valid license or while suspended in Nebraska can result in:
choices: No penalty
Fines and possible jail time
Only a verbal warning
Extra driving privileges
questions.explanation: Driving without a valid license or while suspended is a serious offense that can bring fines and jail time.
